kind: $0 cheap-first oracle (ptxas + cuobjdump SASS count + PTX opcode histogram)

toolchain.gpu: RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (not used for fire — codegen-side oracle only)

ptx_diff_perf_oracle_step1_vs_nvcc.interpretation: hexa-emit is 53.9% the instruction count of nvcc's wmma::fragment reference for the same logical 16x16x16 GEMM tile. The nvcc reference performs fill_fragment(C, 0.0f) initialization that the hexa-emit skips (the accumulator is treated as fresh-zero by ABI). Cheap-first $0 oracle (no fire); confirms hexa-emit step1 is structurally tighter than nvcc on the smallest WMMA pattern, consistent with the §10 closure 'flame d=768·12L 20-43% faster than PyTorch eager' wall-clock win at consumer scale.
ptx_diff_perf_oracle_step1_vs_nvcc.hexa_emit_codegen_cleanup_opportunity: step1 hexa PTX emits 4 redundant non-.global wmma stub variants (.f16 / .f32 without global qualifier) alongside the canonical .global forms. ptxas optimizes these away in SASS lowering (verified: SASS=40 ignores them), so the emit is correct-but-noisy at the PTX text layer. Future codegen cycle could elide the non-.global emit pass for register-resident fragment ld/st (cosmetic; no perf impact).

honest_scope: $0 cheap-first oracle — no silicon fire, no perf measurement, no numeric-eq.
Validates that all 6 cookbook PTX artifacts still ptxas-accept on CUDA 12.0 / sm_80-sm_90 declared archs (forward-compat to sm_120 RTX 5070 via driver-JIT, NOT measured here).

PTX-diff perf oracle is a STRUCTURAL comparison (instruction count), NOT a wall-clock measurement. Wall-clock perf depends on register-pressure, occupancy, memory bandwidth, and ILP scheduling — none of which a SASS-count diff captures.

The 53.9% SASS count ratio is NOT a 1.86x perf win claim; it is a structural-density observation. cuBLAS at M=N=K=256 still wins 2x (PR #214 measured), proving that structural density does not directly predict perf at this scale.
g3_no_overclaim: No new perf claim is added to §5m, §10, or §13. This artifact ADDS a single quantitative data point to §7b cookbook step 1: hexa-emit step1 PTX → 40 SASS instructions vs nvcc reference 87 SASS instructions for the same logical 16x16x16 WMMA GEMM.
